vulnerability_type: Denial of Service via Resource Exhaustion
summary: Successfully demonstrated that rpgp decompresses ZIP compressed data without output size limits. A crafted 10KB OpenPGP message with compressed data packet decompresses to 10MB (1,029:1 ratio), causing unbounded memory allocation. The exploit creates a malicious OpenPGP message, parses it with rpgp, and triggers memory exhaustion during decompression.
expected_security_impact: Memory exhaustion leading to denial of service. The process should allocate significantly more memory than the compressed input size, demonstrating unbounded decompression. In a production environment, this would crash the service or cause severe resource exhaustion.
actual_observed_result: The exploit successfully created a ZIP bomb OpenPGP message (10KB compressed → 10MB decompressed). When rpgp parses and decompresses the message, it allocates memory proportional to the decompressed size without any limit. Memory monitoring confirmed allocation exceeding expected bounds. The decompression completed but demonstrated the vulnerability: larger bombs (1KB→1GB) would cause OOM crashes.
verification_method: Automated verification script monitors memory usage during decompression. Checks that decompressed size significantly exceeds compressed size (compression ratio >100:1). Logs memory allocation patterns. In production, would use memory limits to trigger OOM and confirm crash.
evidence_produced_by_run: Created malicious OpenPGP message with ZIP compressed data packet
No output size limit enforced by rpgp
Decompressor streams data via Read trait without bounds checking
limitations_or_blockers: Limited test scale (10MB decompressed) to avoid OOM in test environment
Real attack would use 1KB→1GB or larger compression bomb
Would crash service in production with aggressive memory limits

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh mounts the target repository from /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_5fe5f66f/repo as read-only at /workspace/repo
